response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer with 15+ years of experience, but his profile is centered on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role requires. His hands-on C++ work dates back to a short 2014–2015 stint at National Instruments, and he shows no exposure to industrial automation, MQTT, IEC 62443, or Secure Development Lifecycle practices. His DLang low-latency and event-driven systems work is adjacent but does not offset the core skills gap for a C++-focused industrial platform role.
strengths: Extensive experience with high-performance, low-latency systems (async I/O, caching, fibers at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Event-driven architecture and distributed platform experience (GISCollective on Kubernetes)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, mentoring, workshops, and open-source contributions
concerns: Minimal recent C++/STL experience; last professional C++ role ended in 2015
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus), or IEC 62443 security background
Career trajectory is web/full-stack, suggesting a mismatch with an embedded/industrial C++ leadership role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or SDL/IEC 62443 experience
Strong systems and performance engineering background
